verified.notes: Nevada Medicaid encompasses multiple programs with different eligibility rules. Search results provide income cap ($2,982/month) for long-term care Medicaid programs only. No authoritative .gov source retrieved with complete asset limits, phone number, or age requirements for the general program. The Assisted Living Waiver specifies age 65+, but this is program-specific, not a universal Nevada Medicaid requirement. Official Nevada Medicaid portal (portaluat.medicaid.nv.gov) did not return detailed eligibility data in search results. Recommend consulting Nevada Division of Social Services or official Nevada Medicaid website for comprehensive current eligibility criteria.

verified.notes: Nevada PACE not launched as of Feb 2026 per NPA data; preparations ongoing per DHHS Aug 2025 announcement. Age is federal PACE standard (55+). No NV-specific eligibility details, income/assets, or phone found in sources.

programs.programName: Nevada Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)

verified.notes: Income limits provided are for QMB program (most restrictive of the three programs). Search results do not specify minimum age requirement for Nevada MSP programs; federal Medicare eligibility typically requires age 65+ or disability/ESRD status, but this is not explicitly stated in Nevada-specific sources retrieved. Official phone number for Nevada DWSS/Medicaid application not found in search results; users directed to contact local Welfare District Office per source [5]. SLMB and QI have higher income limits ($1,478/$1,992 for SLMB; $1,660/$2,239 for QI per source [1]) but QMB values provided as baseline. Asset limits are federal standards applied uniformly across all three programs in Nevada.
